stated_strategic_priorities: Expand FEAUM toward $50B+ through bolt-on acquisitions and organic fund launches across LatAm and selectively into developed markets
Diversify beyond core LatAm franchise by building US GP minority stakes (GPMS) and growth equity capabilities via iGah3 acquisition
Scale private credit and infrastructure platforms to capture LatAm institutional demand for yield-oriented alternatives

contextual_market_factors.tam_notes: LatAm alternative AUM estimated at $150-200B, with Brazil comprising roughly 60% of the addressable pool. Pension fund allocation to alternatives remains well below OECD averages, suggesting structural tailwind for domestic managers with local origination capability.
contextual_market_factors.governance_notes: Dual-class share structure with Class B super-voting shares concentrating control among founders and senior partners. Cayman Islands holding company incorporated ahead of 2021 NASDAQ IPO. CEO Alex Saigh holds significant voting power.
contextual_market_factors.regulatory_notes: Operates under CVM (Comissao de Valores Mobiliarios) regulation in Brazil for fund management. NASDAQ-listed entity domiciled in Cayman Islands. Chilean, Colombian, and other LatAm subsidiaries subject to local securities regulation. US GPMS activity introduces SEC/state-level regulatory considerations.
